mechanism_narrative: A persistently low federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically increasing present equity values and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
evidence_refs: fact:Fed funds at 0.2% as of May 2010
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and 20-day forward returns is not negative and statistically significant (p > 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate above 9.5% indicates severe labor market slack that depresses aggregate consumer spending and corporate revenue expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered observations when UNRATE > 9.5%, or the mean return is not significantly below zero (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous contraction of retail sales and industrial production creates a reinforcing negative feedback loop between consumer demand and manufacturing output that is not captured by either series alone, predicting negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: A linear model with an RSAFS*INDPRO interaction term yields a t-statistic greater than -1.96 on the interaction coefficient over any 3-year rolling window, or the interaction term does not improve out-of-sample R-squared versus additive specifications.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during the early stages of economic recovery signals expanding corporate output and operating leverage, but this positive effect for equities only materializes when the unemployment rate is simultaneously elevated and falling from peak levels.
failure_condition: When UNRATE exceeds 8%, month-over-month INDPRO growth is followed by negative 20-day SPX returns in more than 50% of observations, or the Sharpe ratio of the signal is below 0.2 in this regime.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in real retail sales below zero triggers an immediate reassessment of near-term corporate revenue trajectories, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next week as prices adjust to weaker top-line data.
failure_condition: RSAFS month-over-month declines are followed by positive 5-day SPX returns in more than 55% of instances, or the hit rate of negative returns is not significantly above 50% (binomial p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation captured by a rising CPI index erodes real corporate earnings expectations and increases the probability of future monetary tightening, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month CPIAUCSL changes show a positive Rank IC with 20-day SPX forward returns over a 3-year rolling window, or accelerating inflation coincides with positive returns in more than 55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained nonfarm payroll growth indicates expanding household income and aggregate demand, which translates into positive earnings revisions and supports S&P 500 appreciation over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Three-month change in PAYEMS shows negative correlation with 60-day SPX forward returns (Rank IC less than 0) over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts expand while industrial production is simultaneously contracting, this divergence indicates a sectoral rotation toward domestic construction that is insufficient to offset manufacturing weakness, resulting in weak S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The joint condition of HOUST increasing and INDPRO decreasing produces positive 5-day SPX returns in more than 50% of observations, or the interaction term in a regression is not negative and significant (p > 0.05).
